hero_097.earlyGame: In the early game, prioritize using Skill 1's Strafe to pressure enemies while accumulating Fury. Once your Fury is maxed and you enter the Excited state, skill cooldowns are reduced, and Skill 1 can pierce through to damage enemies behind the target, allowing you to clear minions and harass simultaneously. However, since your Movement Speed decreases during Strafe, be careful of enemy approaches and ganks.
hero_097.midGame: In the mid game, increasing your Attack Speed will enhance Skill 1's fire rate and reduce its cooldown, significantly boosting your damage output. Use Skill 2's Heavy Cannon to slow enemies, taking advantage of its wall reflections and splitting shotgun blasts to gain the upper hand in narrow spaces. When allies initiate a fight, it's crucial to hold your Ultimate to increase your Movement Speed and expand your search range, providing long-range missile support to secure kills.
hero_097.lateGame: In the late game, the key is to keep your Fury high and quickly enter the Excited state during teamfights. In the Excited state, you recover HP whenever your Fury exceeds the cap, enhancing your sustained combat ability. Your Ultimate's wide-range missile support is extremely powerful in teamfights, but since your mobility is relatively low, make sure to secure a safe position behind your allies to keep up Skill 1's Strafe.
hero_097.teamfight: In teamfights, activate your Ultimate in sync with your allies' attacks to rain down a massive amount of missiles and deal damage to the entire enemy team. In the Excited state, the missile firing interval is shortened and Skill 2's scatter shots increase, allowing you to unleash overwhelming AoE burst damage. Your role is to survive tenaciously by utilizing the HP recovery effect when Fury exceeds the limit, continuously dealing damage and wearing down enemies with Skill 1.
hero_097.commonMistakes: If you start a fight without managing your Fury, your skills won't be enhanced, and you won't reach your full damage potential. Also, be aware that your Movement Speed drops by 50 during Skill 1's Strafe, making it harder to dodge powerful enemy skills and CC. Since your Ultimate's missiles only fire when allies attack, activating it while isolated or when allies cannot engage often wastes the skill.
hero_097.strengths: Your strengths lie in the massive skill enhancements from the Excited state and the high sustained combat ability from HP recovery when Fury exceeds the limit. Increasing Attack Speed directly translates to reduced skill cooldowns and increased fire rates, allowing you to efficiently utilize basic attack-oriented stats despite being skill-dependent. Additionally, the ultra-long-range missile support from your Ultimate is excellent for providing powerful cover fire for allies even from a distance.
hero_097.weaknesses: Your weakness is that your skills have low performance when Fury is not accumulated, making you highly dependent on the Excited state. While using Skill 1, your Movement Speed is reduced, making it hard to dodge enemy skills. Since you lack mobility skills to compensate, escaping close-quarters combat is extremely difficult. Also, because your Ultimate's missiles rely on allied attacks, it cannot show its true value in situations where you cannot coordinate with your team.

hero_098.earlyGame: In the early game, focus on farming and sustaining with Skill 2. Hit multiple enemies or minions with Skill 2 to stack marks, and maintain your HP while dealing damage with the enhanced basic attack's continuous dashes. Utilize the Movement Speed boost from Skill 1's Dive mode for quick rotations between lanes and to effectively roam, ensuring you finish off enemies dropping below 30% HP (the target of your Passive's additional true damage).
hero_098.midGame: In the mid game, use your high mobility to actively roam the map and target isolated or low-HP enemies. The combo of diving with Skill 1 and launching a surprise attack with Skill 2 or your Ultimate is highly effective. If you cast your Ultimate while in Dive mode, you can drag enemies in your path to the endpoint. Use this to pull enemies toward your allies or block their escape routes, racking up kills and taking control of the match.
hero_098.lateGame: In late game teamfights, the standard approach is to wait for your allies to whittle down the enemies' HP before going in, maximizing your Passive's true damage. Approach from the enemy's blind spot in Dive mode, and use your Ultimate to drag multiple enemies, disrupting their formation. In the middle of the enemy formation, continuously chain Skill 2 and enhanced basic attacks to cycle heals and cooldown reductions, demonstrating incredible tenacity.
hero_098.teamfight: In teamfights, initiating or splitting the enemy formation by using your Ultimate's multi-target drag from Skill 1's Dive mode is extremely powerful. Hit Skill 2 in dense enemy areas to quickly max out your mark stacks, and maneuver with the continuous pursuit and HP recovery of your enhanced basic attacks. Since true damage triggers when an enemy's HP drops below 30%, it is crucial to prioritize low-HP targets, securing kills to increase Skill 2's rotation rate.
hero_098.commonMistakes: If you charge headfirst into the enemy team, your low durability puts you at risk of being instantly burst down upon receiving CC. Additionally, failing to manage Skill 2's mark stacks and not properly looping your enhanced basic attack dashes, healing, and cooldown reductions will prevent you from utilizing the hero's sustained combat strength. Focusing only on high-HP enemies where your Passive's true damage won't trigger is also inefficient and should be avoided.
hero_098.strengths: Your greatest strengths are the extremely high mobility from Skill 1's Dive and the damage output and self-sustain from looping Skill 2 with enhanced basic attacks. Furthermore, your Passive deals true damage to enemies below 30% HP, making you an exceptionally effective Assassin at finishing off targets with a sliver of health left. The forced displacement from your Ultimate while in Dive mode also contributes greatly to disrupting enemy formations in teamfights.
hero_098.weaknesses: Since your survivability relies on healing, you have a glaring weakness: if enemies hit you with strong CC (like stuns or suppression) and break your skill and basic attack loop, you can be killed without being able to do anything. Also, your healing is halved against non-hero units, reducing your tenacity when only minions or monsters are around. You also struggle to solo kill high-HP enemies since your Passive won't activate.

hero_099.earlyGame: In the early game, it's crucial to use Skill 1 and Skill 2 to unleash dual attacks from both your main body and your mirror image, consuming Passive marks to reset your cooldowns. Rack up damage with Skill 1's dash and enhanced basic attacks, while using Skill 2's AoE damage to heal yourself and clear waves or jungle camps. Rapidly cycling your skills is key to utilizing the execution damage based on the target's lost HP when triggering marks.
hero_099.midGame: In the mid game, leverage your mobility to actively initiate ganks. Your strength lies in your burst damage: apply marks by hitting enemies with your main body and mirror image, and instantly finish them off with continuous attacks using cooldown resets. Once you learn your Ultimate's swap between your main body and mirror image, you can dodge enemy attacks while striking from unpredictable angles, so actively aim for ambushes from outside their vision and tower dives.
hero_099.lateGame: In the late game, your primary role is to securely assassinate low-durability backline targets like Marksmen and Mages. Flank or approach from behind in teamfights, instantly consume marks with your main body and mirror image combo, and secure kills with damage based on their lost HP. Mastering your Ultimate's swap to spread the enemy's focus, ensure your own safety, and perform hit-and-run tactics is essential.
hero_099.teamfight: In teamfights, avoid charging straight down the middle; instead, aim for the moment the enemy formation breaks or when their backline is isolated. Cast your skills to summon your mirror image, scattering AoE damage and marks, and utilize skill resets to unleash a continuous barrage of skills. When powerful enemy skills or CC come your way, use your Ultimate's 'Mirror Illusion' to swap positions with your mirror image to dodge them, fighting while thoroughly confusing the enemy.
hero_099.commonMistakes: If you fail to hit enemies with both your main body and mirror image to fully consume marks, your cooldowns won't reset, causing your damage to plummet. Additionally, forgetting that Skill 2 slows your own Movement Speed upon cast and using it while fleeing can backfire and allow enemies to catch up. You must also be mindful of the 5-second cooldown on the reset effect for the same target.
hero_099.strengths: Your greatest strengths are the overwhelming burst damage from your main body and mirror image, and the skill cooldown reset mechanic from consuming marks. Because you deal execution damage based on lost HP, you excel exceptionally at finishing off enemies. Moreover, Skill 2 provides sustain through HP recovery, and your Ultimate's position swap allows for a highly flexible playstyle where you can confuse enemies, deal damage, and disengage safely.
hero_099.weaknesses: Since you rely on skill cooldown resets, any mechanical mistake or enemy dodge that prevents you from consuming a mark will instantly leave you defenseless. Furthermore, because Skill 2 reduces your own Movement Speed when used, poor positioning will leave you vulnerable. With low durability, if your Ultimate swaps are predicted and you are hit with CC, your fragility will be exposed, and you will be taken down in an instant.

hero_100.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize your Passive to quickly farm by getting close to jungle monsters and setting them free to gain buffs. Pick up the seed bombs dropped from your basic attacks to increase your Movement Speed and Attack Speed, enhancing your jungle clear efficiency. When ganking a lane, use Skill 2's rolling dash with Qiuqiu to hit and stun enemies, then rack up damage with your enhanced basic attack seed bombs.
hero_100.midGame: In the mid game, unleash the power of the monsters you've gathered. Use Skill 1 to grow flora and summon animal companions, pushing towers and gaining the upper hand in teamfights alongside your allies. Within Skill 1's AoE, your Attack Speed and Movement Speed increase, and your HP recovers, making territorial combat extremely powerful. By bouncing off walls with Skill 2, you can execute technical ambushes utilizing stuns and damage amplification.
hero_100.lateGame: In the late game, stick with your allies and act as a Support/Tank, elevating your team's overall combat power with Skill 1's AoE buffs and monster summons. Engaging the enemy carries by casting your Ultimate to make Qiuqiu jump to a designated location, dealing massive damage and a stun to disrupt their formation, is crucial. While Qiuqiu fights independently, your main body is squishy, so provide cover fire with seed bombs from a safe distance.
hero_100.teamfight: In teamfights, your primary role is to dominate the battlefield by granting powerful AoE buffs and healing to your allies and summoned monsters with Skill 1. Target dense enemy clusters with your Ultimate to send Qiuqiu charging in, providing an AoE stun to spark the engagement. Skill 2 can negate one hard CC, allowing you to slip past enemy disruptions and wreak havoc in their backline. Pay close attention to the positioning of your main body while separated from Qiuqiu.
hero_100.commonMistakes: A common mistake is forgetting that your main body has extremely low durability after detaching Qiuqiu with your Ultimate; stepping too far forward will result in instant death. Additionally, be careful of misdirecting your Skill 2 roll by hitting a wall and bouncing in an unintended direction, causing you to miss your stun or end up stranded deep within enemy lines. Engaging without setting monsters free to stack your buffs will prevent you from unleashing your true power.
hero_100.strengths: Your unique mechanic of setting monsters free without killing them allows for safe and unconventional jungle clearing. With Skill 1's powerful AoE buffs and healing for allies, along with summoning multiple monsters to fight alongside you, you excel exceptionally at pushing and controlling teamfights. Your Ultimate's long-range AoE stun with Qiuqiu is a potent initiation tool, and Skill 2 provides mobility and disruption while offering CC immunity.
hero_100.weaknesses: After detaching Qiuqiu with your Ultimate, your main body has very low stats and is highly vulnerable to Assassins. If Qiuqiu is defeated, it takes time to return to combat, significantly reducing your fighting power during that window. Additionally, if you haven't gathered enough jungle monsters, the benefits of Skill 1 are weak; if the enemy controls the game's pace and invades your jungle, it becomes very difficult to recover.

hero_101.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize your Passive's basic attack firefly summons to dominate the laning phase by dealing magic damage, harassing enemies by revealing their stealth, and healing your allies. When there are no enemies around, charge your basic attack to heal your own HP and maintain durability. Keep track of your 'Power of Fireflies' stacks, which accumulate every 1.5 seconds; casting Skill 1 with high stacks will deal massive damage and an AoE knockup.
hero_101.midGame: In the mid game, use Skill 2 to fly between bushes, utilizing your high mobility to traverse the map, support allies, and scout for enemies. When moving through bushes with Skill 2, fireflies automatically attack and reveal enemies or heal allies, making it extremely powerful in skirmishes. To prepare for fights in areas without bushes, summon a 'Bush of Light' with your Ultimate, and control the area with continuous AoE damage and healing from the emitted fireflies.
hero_101.lateGame: In the late game, a fully stacked wide-range Skill 1 knockup becomes a crucial CC that can decide the outcome of teamfights. Against enemy Assassins with stealth, use your Passive or skills to hit them with fireflies, applying a 3-second stealth reveal to protect your backline allies. Place your Ultimate's 'Bush of Light' along enemy advance routes or near objectives to secure vision and provide continuous support.
hero_101.teamfight: In teamfights, the ideal approach is to use a fully stacked Skill 1 to knock up multiple enemies for an initiation or counter-engage, while simultaneously restoring a significant amount of your allies' HP. Place your Ultimate in the center of the battlefield to fire fireflies at up to 5 surrounding heroes every second, providing continuous support across the frontlines. Use Skill 2 to dart between natural bushes and your summoned Bush of Light, dodging enemy attacks while maintaining an advantageous position.
hero_101.commonMistakes: Since Skill 1's range and additional effects (damage and healing) depend on your 'Power of Fireflies' stacks, a common mistake is rushing to cast it without stacks, resulting in a weak impact. Additionally, while charging your basic attack to heal yourself in areas without enemies, healing for your allies is interrupted; abandoning your allies to prioritize your own recovery during a fight can easily cause you to lose the teamfight.
hero_101.strengths: Your strength is a highly versatile Support toolkit capable of healing allies, damaging enemies, applying knockups, revealing stealth, and securing vision. The fireflies from your Passive and skills automatically track targets, ensuring reliable support. Thanks to your bush-to-bush mobility with Skill 2 and bush-summoning with your Ultimate, you possess extremely high map mobility, survivability, and area control in local skirmishes by utilizing the terrain.
hero_101.weaknesses: To maximize the effect of your core Skill 1, you must spend time accumulating 'Power of Fireflies' stacks, meaning you cannot chain powerful skills in quick succession. Since part of your mobility and support relies on 'bushes', you struggle in open areas when your Ultimate is on cooldown and no natural bushes are nearby. You are also vulnerable to sudden burst damage or continuous hard CC, and you have a high dependency on your allies.

hero_102.earlyGame: In the early game, poke enemies with Skill 1's ranged attack while utilizing the 80% Movement Speed boost on hit to maintain a safe distance. Focus on hitting enemies with your skills to unlock 'Additional Skills' and build up your Passive mark stacks. Use Skill 2's dash and heal to gain an advantage in lane trades, and make it your basic strategy to unleash massive damage all at once with your enhanced basic attack 'Splash Graduation' when you reach 3 stacks.
hero_102.midGame: In the mid game, your true power shines as you acquire cooldown reduction items and increase your skill rotation rate. Continuously hit skills to maintain your 'Additional Skills', and apply the Wound effect's Attack Speed reduction to win brawls against physical attackers. While casting your enhanced basic attack, you gain damage reduction, which, when combined with your Ultimate's Super Armor, allows you to endure enemy CC and burst damage.
hero_102.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, utilize your high durability and sustained damage to dive deep into the enemy backline and relentlessly hunt down squishy Marksmen and Mages. Seamlessly chain your skills and enhanced basic attacks, ensuring kills with additional damage based on the target's lost HP. Even when drawing enemy focus, you must display incredible tenacity using the damage reduction from your enhanced basic attacks and your Ultimate, along with Skill 2's healing, to hold the frontline.
hero_102.teamfight: In teamfights, ideally, you should prepare by hitting minions or jungle monsters to build your mark stacks so you can enter the fray with your enhanced basic attack ready. Dash into the enemy formation with Skill 2, use your Ultimate's AoE damage and Movement Speed slow to pin them down, and unleash the 7-hit combo of your enhanced basic attack. It's crucial to apply the Attack Speed reduction debuff from your Additional Skills to the enemy carries, drastically cutting their damage output.
hero_102.commonMistakes: If you fail to hit enemies with your skills and remain stuck on 'Starting Skills', you won't build Passive marks or trigger your enhanced basic attack, causing your damage to plummet. Another common mistake is charging defenselessly into the enemy team without the damage reduction or Super Armor from your enhanced basic attack or your Ultimate, resulting in instant death. Neglecting to manage Wound stacks and fighting head-on without reducing the enemy's Attack Speed is also dangerous.
hero_102.strengths: Your greatest strengths are your flowing combo attacks initiated by skill hits, and the percentage damage and damage reduction provided by your enhanced basic attack 'Splash Graduation'. The Attack Speed reduction debuff from your Additional Skills gives you an overwhelming advantage against basic attack-reliant heroes. With Skill 2's high healing and your Ultimate's Super Armor, you boast extremely high sustained combat ability and dueling power, making you hard to kill even when diving the enemy team.
hero_102.weaknesses: Because you must consistently hit skills to perform optimally, your mechanical difficulty is high, and you struggle against highly evasive or long-range enemies. Since you rely heavily on skill combos, being hit by a Silence will shut you down completely. Additionally, the Wound effect is removed when the enemy lands a basic attack, making the debuff less effective against heroes with slow, heavy-hitting attacks or Mages.

hero_103.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize Skill 1's knockup and Skill 2's charged dash to trade damage while applying CC to your enemies. Since every skill use grants a Passive Resonance stack, it's crucial to adjust your stack count according to the situation and unleash the most optimal Resonance Skill (enhanced basic attack). Consume stacks to reduce the cooldowns of Skill 1 and Skill 2, dominating the lane and jungle by overwhelming your opponent with rapid strikes.
hero_103.midGame: In the mid game, use your mobility and strong CC to actively initiate ganks. Charge Skill 2 to boost your Movement Speed, quickly close the gap, and slow enemies with a sweep. Follow up with Skill 1's knockup, then consume your Resonance stacks for massive damage. Once you learn your Ultimate, you can continuously knock up enemies in a wide area while gaining Super Armor and damage reduction, drastically improving your control in skirmishes.
hero_103.lateGame: In the late game, coordinate with your allies and take on the role of precisely engaging the enemy carries. Disrupt the enemy formation with your Ultimate's back-and-forth knockups and continuous thrusts, relying on Super Armor and damage reduction to survive focused fire. Afterward, deploy the 2nd phase 'Roar of the Spear' to sweep and create a burning area, zoning enemies with continuous magic damage while endlessly spamming enhanced basic attacks to cycle your cooldowns.
hero_103.teamfight: In teamfights, the timing and placement of your Ultimate will largely decide the outcome. Target dense enemy clusters with your Ultimate to knock up multiple targets and set up kills for your team. During your Ultimate, you are in a Super Armor state, allowing you to force your way through enemy CC. Depending on the situation, cancel Skill 2's dash to immediately trigger the 2nd phase burn, cutting off the enemy's escape while continuously dealing damage with stack-consuming basic attacks.
hero_103.commonMistakes: Failing to manage your Passive Resonance stacks and haphazardly using the wrong enhanced basic attack for the situation will prevent you from maximizing your damage and utility. Also, beware of exposing yourself to enemy attacks while charging Skill 2, or misjudging your dash distance and ending up stranded deep within enemy lines. Overestimating your Ultimate's powerful damage reduction and Super Armor by diving too deep without allied follow-up should also be avoided.
hero_103.strengths: Your strength lies in your highly adaptable playstyle, made possible by a variety of enhanced basic attacks dependent on your skill usage count. Since triggering enhanced basic attacks reduces skill cooldowns, you boast a very high attack rate and excellent sustained combat ability. The multiple knockups from Skill 1 and your Ultimate, combined with the Super Armor and damage reduction during your Ultimate, grant you extremely high initiation power and survivability in teamfights.
hero_103.weaknesses: Because managing multiple skill combos and Resonance stacks is mandatory, the mechanical difficulty is very high, meaning player proficiency directly dictates your performance. Since you rely on a flurry of strikes, your instant burst damage is somewhat lower compared to traditional Assassins. Additionally, your teamfight presence drops while your powerful Ultimate is on cooldown, so if enemies dodge your skills or shut you down with hard CC before you cast, you will die without showing your tenacity.

hero_104.earlyGame: In the early game, take advantage of your Passive wall-climbing and ranged basic attacks to safely clear the jungle and harass lanes. On top of walls, your vision expands, and the overwhelming mobility of jumping from wall to wall with Skill 2 allows you to ambush enemies from unpredictable routes. Drop down from a wall with Skill 1 to knock up the enemy, using the enhanced melee basic attack's bonus damage and HP recovery to gain the upper hand in early skirmishes.
hero_104.midGame: In the mid game, use your fully stacked Skill 2 to quickly traverse the entire map, aggressively hunting down isolated or low-HP enemies. The combo of applying marks to enemies in a wide area from a wall with your Ultimate, then dropping down with Skill 1 to deliver CC and enhanced basic attacks, is highly effective. Don't miss the moment an enemy's HP drops; activate your Ultimate's 2nd phase to burst the mark, ignoring untargetability and securing a guaranteed kill with the execute effect.
hero_104.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, to compensate for your low durability, you must avoid frontal engagements and instead scout the enemy formation from atop the walls. Once your allies engage and the enemies' HP is whittled down, launch a surprise attack from the wall. Apply marks to multiple enemies with your Ultimate's wide sweep, and fulfill your role as an Assassin by instantly executing enemies below 1/8 HP with 'Jade-ify'. After securing kills, retreat back to the walls to enforce hit-and-run tactics.
hero_104.teamfight: In teamfights, secure vision from the safety of the walls and wait for an opportunity to strike the enemy backline. Drop down with Skill 1 to disrupt their formation with a knockup, and control the distance using the ranged knockback from your enhanced basic attacks. Most importantly, your use of your Ultimate's execute effect is key; after spreading marks to the entire enemy team, you are expected to act as a finisher, wiping out low-HP targets.
hero_104.commonMistakes: While walls offer excellent mobility and vision, they come with a hefty penalty: the duration of any Movement Speed slows or CC you receive is increased by 25%. A common mistake is taking a fatal blow on a wall because you caught an enemy disruption skill. Also, neglecting Skill 2's stack management and getting stranded on the ground without a way back up leaves you extremely vulnerable. Over-fixating on your Ultimate's execute effect and casting it before the enemy's HP is low will result in lacking damage.
hero_104.strengths: Your greatest strength is the overwhelming mobility and unparalleled ambush routes provided by your Passive wall-walking and Skill 2, things no other hero can do. Combined with the broad vision from atop walls, your Ultimate's ability to ignore untargetability and unconditionally execute enemies below 1/8 HP poses a massive threat. The versatility between Skill 1's knockup and your enhanced basic attacks allows you to flexibly adapt to both melee and ranged combat situations.
hero_104.weaknesses: If you are hit by CC while on a wall, the penalty extends its duration, heavily restricting your options against enemies with hard CC. Because you specialize in mobility, your base durability is low, making you extremely weak in straightforward ground brawls. Furthermore, to fully utilize your Ultimate's powerful execute effect, you need your allies to poke down the enemies' HP first, as you lack the sustained firepower to burst them down from full health alone.

hero_105.earlyGame: In the early game, use your Passive's AoE damage and max HP% damage to clear minions and harass the enemy at the same time. Activate Skill 1 to enhance your next 3 basic attacks, dominating the lane with a wider AoE and higher percentage damage. When ambushed, it's crucial to use Skill 2's smokescreen to grant stealth to yourself and your allies while jumping into the air to safely disengage, ignoring terrain.
hero_105.midGame: In the mid game, utilize your aerial mobility to deal damage from advantageous positions. Jump into the air with Skill 2, cast basic attacks or skills to extend your hang time, leap over walls, and attack from unpredictable angles. Since basic attacks from the air cannot be blocked by grounded enemy heroes, you can directly snipe the enemy carries in the back. In skirmishes, unleash Skill 1's enhanced attacks to rapidly melt enemy HP.
hero_105.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, you act as the primary carry, utilizing your range and hovering abilities to constantly pump out massive damage from safety. If you draw enemy focus, use your Ultimate to jump high into the air, gaining Super Armor to ignore CC while staying in the fight. Your Ultimate's missiles deal additional damage based on the target's lost HP, so use it to secure kills on fleeing or low-HP enemies at the end of a fight.
hero_105.teamfight: In teamfights, your basic strategy is to sit behind your allies and rain down the AoE damage of your Skill 1-enhanced basic attacks onto the enemy formation. If an enemy Assassin approaches, use Skill 2's smokescreen to stealth with your allies, while you take to the skies to counterattack using the terrain advantage. Once the enemy's HP is low, activate your Ultimate to lock onto an enemy hero and bombard them with 5 missiles for massive damage, delivering the decisive blow in the teamfight.
hero_105.commonMistakes: While on the ground, your basic attack projectiles will be blocked by the closest enemy heroes, so a common mistake is having your damage endlessly absorbed by the enemy Tank without ever hurting the backline. Also, you are not invincible while in the air via Skill 2 or your Ultimate; your mid-air movements can be predicted, leaving you at risk of being shot down by anti-air attacks or ranged skills. Using the smokescreen's stealth only for your own escape instead of protecting allies is also a waste.
hero_105.strengths: Your strength lies in your basic attacks, which deal AoE damage and max HP% damage, allowing you to deal high damage even against Tanks. Skill 2's smokescreen stealth and terrain-ignoring aerial movement grant you overwhelming survivability and the freedom to position anywhere. Attacking from the air bypasses enemy blocking, letting you accurately target their backline. With your Ultimate's Super Armor and execution missiles based on lost HP, your finishing power is also extremely high.
hero_105.weaknesses: Since much of your mobility relies on aerial movement, you become very fragile during ground combat when Skill 2 and your Ultimate are on cooldown, making escape difficult. Grounded basic attacks are easily blocked by the enemy frontline, making it hard to concentrate damage on your intended target. Furthermore, your Ultimate's missiles can be intercepted by other enemy heroes, meaning you might fail to finish off your primary target in chaotic fights.

hero_106.earlyGame: In the early game, seize control of the lane by utilizing the continuous severing damage of your Moonblade from your Passive and Skill 1. Apply the Moonblade to enemies with basic attacks to safely harass them from a distance while securing vision. Casting Skill 1 increases your Movement Speed by 45% and allows you to cross walls, enabling you to dodge attacks and trade damage from a safe position. Since the Moonblade's attacks reduce Skill 1's cooldown, it's crucial to keep slicing the enemy.
hero_106.midGame: In the mid game, dominate mid-range combat using the poke and slow effects of Skill 2's Fire Snake. Dash the snake from a designated location towards yourself to root the enemy, combining it with the Moonblade for continuous damage. If you use Skill 2 without holding the Moonblade, its cooldown is halved, so using it contextually is key. Increasing your Attack Speed shortens Skill 2's cast animation, allowing for smoother maneuvers.
hero_106.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, knowing when to use your Ultimate and its powerful 'Blindness' debuff will decide the match. Hit your Ultimate on basic attack-reliant enemy carries or heroes with powerful skills to negate their basic attack damage and deflect flying skills, drastically reducing their firepower. Simultaneously, enter your Ultimate's Self-Enhancement mode, accumulating energy by dealing and taking damage, and collapse the enemy formation with an AoE knockup and damage.
hero_106.teamfight: In teamfights, charging into the enemy formation with your Ultimate's Fire Snake to apply Blindness to multiple enemies and strip away their offensive options serves as an incredibly potent initiation. Instantly recall your Moonblade upon casting, and by fighting in the middle of the enemy pack during your 6-second Self-Enhancement, rapidly build energy to trigger continuous AoE knockups. Since you'll be taking damage as you fight, ideally you should go on a rampage while receiving healing and shield support from allies.
hero_106.commonMistakes: Because you rely on the continuous damage from the Moonblade, creating too much distance after exhausting your skills and letting the severing effect drop will drastically cut your damage and skill rotation rate. While your Ultimate's Blindness is immensely powerful, using it on melee Mages or Tanks without basic attacks or flying skills yields little benefit. Be careful not to charge blindly into the enemy team to stack energy during Self-Enhancement only to get killed before the knockup triggers.
hero_106.strengths: Your strengths include the Moonblade's auto-tracking continuous damage and vision, along with the tenacious playstyle enabled by Skill 1's mobility. Your greatest weapon is the 'Blindness' effect from your Ultimate; the ability to negate enemy basic attack damage and deflect skills provides an overwhelming counter against specific Marksmen and Mages. In your Ultimate's Self-Enhancement state, your offensive and defensive energy-stacking AoE knockups boast enough control to single-handedly disrupt teamfights.
hero_106.weaknesses: Since you lack instantaneous burst damage, you struggle with Assassin-like playstyles of rapidly deleting an enemy's HP. Furthermore, to maximize the knockups during your Ultimate's Self-Enhancement, you must risk taking hits and stay close to the enemy team; without defensive items or allied follow-up, you risk being bursted down instantly. If the enemy team composition has many heroes unaffected by Blindness, your core strengths will be heavily diluted.

hero_107.earlyGame: In the early game, actively trade damage on the frontline using the recovery capability of your 'Low HP Mode (Gray Damage)' Passive. You excel in prolonged lane battles, as you can recover taken damage by attacking enemy heroes or minions. Use Skill 1's double jump to clear minions and harass, utilizing Super Armor to ignore enemy CC while anchoring the frontline and creating favorable situations.
hero_107.midGame: In the mid game, utilize the powerful terrain control of Skill 2 and your Ultimate to shine in skirmishes and ganks. Catch enemies in narrow jungle corridors or near walls, and use Skill 2's rake to smash them against the wall, dealing a stun and massive damage. Your enhanced basic attack after casting Skill 2 can also pull enemies in, ensuring no one escapes. A strong tactic is to block their retreat with your Ultimate's barrier and coordinate with allies to secure kills.
hero_107.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, stand at the very front of your team as the primary Tank, boasting overwhelming durability. Your biggest role is to absorb all enemy focus and survive tenaciously by recovering lost HP through Skill 1's AoE attacks and basic attacks. The combo of deploying your Ultimate's barrier to split the formation when enemies group up, followed immediately by Skill 2 for a wall-slam, is incredibly effective. Corner the enemy carries against walls to set up an environment where your allies can deal damage.
hero_107.teamfight: In teamfights, dominate the battlefield with forceful initiations combining your Ultimate's dividing barrier and Skill 2's stun. Placing a barrier in the enemy's escape path and instantly hitting it with Skill 2 to stun them is a highly powerful technique. Once you dive deep into the enemy formation, disrupt them with Skill 1's Super Armor and AoE knockup while maintaining your HP with your Passive's recovery, wasting the enemy's time and skills as an unkillable Tank.
hero_107.commonMistakes: A common mistake is overestimating your Passive's recovery and taking too much damage without being able to land counterattacks; the HP in Low HP Mode will gradually drain, leading to your death. Also, because your Ultimate's barrier blocks your allies' path as well, poor placement or timing can cause disasters (effectively trolling) by cutting off fleeing allies or blocking your attackers' line of sight.
hero_107.strengths: Your greatest strength is possessing some of the most astonishing durability and tenacity of any hero, thanks to a Passive that recovers taken damage by attacking. With Super Armor on Skill 1 and your Ultimate, you can ignore enemy CC, making you an exceptionally elite frontline. Moreover, combining the terrain creation of your Ultimate with the terrain-dependent stun and pull of Skill 2 allows you to unleash powerful control abilities like isolation and CC.
hero_107.weaknesses: Since your durability depends on recovering HP by dealing damage, your core tenacity severely drops if enemies buy anti-heal items. With low mobility and short range, you struggle immensely against Marksmen and Mages who can constantly kite you from afar. Because you cannot output burst damage, taking down an enemy alone takes time; without allied fire support, you cannot fully fulfill your role as a Tank.

hero_108.earlyGame: In the early game, balancing your Passive's ranged and melee basic attacks is crucial. From afar, bounce your Flying Blades to accumulate stacks while safely farming and harassing. When an enemy gets close, unleash the melee 3-hit combo to deliver high burst damage. After landing Skill 1's AoE damage and slow, utilize the Attack Speed boost from the Frenzy state to increase the rotation rate of your Flying Blades, establishing an early advantage in lane trades.
hero_108.midGame: In the mid game, aim for kills in skirmishes and ganks by relying on Skill 2's high mobility and continuous strikes. Dash in with Skill 2, catching enemies in front of you to trigger 'Featherstorm' (an 8-hit combo), fighting tenaciously with its damage and HP recovery. Poking with basic attacks beforehand is key, as hitting Skill 2 with full Passive marks triggers additional damage. If the enemy's counterattack is too fierce, recast Skill 2 to cancel and disengage.
hero_108.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, aggressive engagements utilizing your Ultimate's 'Unyielding State' and Revive ability are extremely powerful. Dive right into the enemy formation, cast your Ultimate to gain temporary HP and Movement Speed, and run rampant with Skill 2's continuous attacks while enduring focused fire. Even if you fall, as long as your Ultimate is not on cooldown, you will revive on the spot, dealing massive AoE damage and rejoining the fight, allowing you to boldly push the frontline and create opportunities for your allies.
hero_108.teamfight: In teamfights, the timing of your Ultimate and your handling of the Passive Flying Blade bounces will dictate the flow of battle. During your Ultimate's Unyielding state, strike the enemy's key player with Skill 2's continuous attacks, aiming for a kill with the final additional damage. Because healing effects are accumulated over the 4 seconds of the Unyielding state and applied when it ends, your chances of surviving despite taking damage are high. You are also an excellent decoy, using your Revive passive to force initiations and bait out powerful enemy skills.
hero_108.commonMistakes: Be careful not to forget the mechanic that prevents ranged attacks before the Flying Blade returns to your hand, leaving you defenseless if enemies close in. Additionally, if you recklessly charge in overestimating your Ultimate's temporary HP and the shield is completely broken before the 4 seconds are up, the Unyielding state immediately ends and you will die easily. Charging into the enemy team assuming you can revive, without realizing your Ultimate is on cooldown, is also a fatal misplay.
hero_108.strengths: Your strength is the ability to fight at various ranges thanks to a unique basic attack that combines long-range poke with close-range burst, along with Skill 2's mobile, self-healing attacks. Your greatest weapons are the massive temporary durability boost (Unyielding State) from your Ultimate and the AoE damage Revive ability that triggers upon death. This allows you to disrupt the enemy team like a Tank despite being an Assassin/Fighter, and even if you go down, you immediately become a threat once more.
hero_108.weaknesses: Your mechanical difficulty is extremely high, requiring significant player skill to manage the Passive Flying Blade bounces and choose the right attack depending on distance. You are highly dependent on your Ultimate's Unyielding state and Revive, making you very fragile and unable to effectively participate in teamfights while it's on cooldown. You also struggle against enemies with enough burst damage to instantly shred your Ultimate's temporary HP, or team comps that can reliably chain CC on you during your post-revive vulnerability window.
